title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement [INSERT CONTACT METHOD]. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.0, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/0/ code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https:// www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to kootlake
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to kootlake
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed).

title: Contributing to kootlake
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("", fork = TRUE). Install development dependences devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. Begin commit merge messages - indicate user facing changes included NEWS.md. use fledge automatically generate NEWS.md file.

title: Contributing to kootlake
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to kootlake
text: Please note kootlake project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: Getting help with kootlake
text: Thanks using kootlake! filing issue, places explore pieces put together make process smooth possible.

title: Getting help with kootlake
text: opening new issue, sure search issues pull requests make sure bug hasn’t reported /already fixed development version. default, search pre-populated :issue :open. can edit qualifiers (e.g. :pr, :closed) needed. example, ’d simply remove :open search issues repo, open closed.

title: Getting help with kootlake
text: Start making minimal reproducible example using reprex package. haven’t heard used reprex , ’re treat! Seriously, reprex make R-question-asking endeavors easier (pretty insane ROI five ten minutes ’ll take learn ’s ). additional reprex pointers, check Get help! section tidyverse site.

title: Allometry of Kootenay Lake Rainbow Trout
code: library(ggplot2) library(broom)  fish <- kootlake::fish  is.na(fish$Weight[is.na(fish$Weight) | fish$Weight == 0]) <- TRUE  WL_mod <- lm(log(Weight) ~ log(Length), data = fish) WL_data <- data.frame(Length = seq(min(fish$Length), max(fish$Length), length.out = 30L)) WL_data$Weight <- exp(predict(WL_mod, newdata = WL_data))  ggplot(data = fish, aes(x = Length, y = Weight)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1) +   geom_line(data = WL_data, col = "blue") +   expand_limits(y = 0) tidy(WL_mod, conf.int = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   term        estimate std.error statistic p.value conf.low conf.high #>   <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 (Intercept)   -19.0     0.0954     -199.       0   -19.1     -18.8  #> 2 log(Length)     3.10    0.0150      207.       0     3.07      3.13

title: Allometry of Kootenay Lake Rainbow Trout
code: FL_mod <- lm(log(Fecundity) ~ log(Length), data = fish)  FL_data <- data.frame(Length = seq(min(fish$Length), max(fish$Length), length.out = 30L)) FL_data$Fecundity <- exp(predict(FL_mod, newdata = FL_data))  ggplot(data = fish, aes(x = Length, y = Fecundity)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +   geom_line(data = FL_data, col = "blue") +   expand_limits(y = 0) tidy(FL_mod, conf.int = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   term        estimate std.error statistic  p.value conf.low conf.high #>   <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 (Intercept)   -10.6      0.847     -12.5 4.87e-21   -12.3      -8.91 #> 2 log(Length)     2.94     0.132      22.2 2.20e-37     2.68      3.20

title: Allometry of Kootenay Lake Rainbow Trout
code: FW_mod <- lm(log(Fecundity) ~ log(Weight), data = fish)  FW_data <- data.frame(Weight = seq(min(fish$Weight, na.rm = TRUE), max(fish$Weight, na.rm = TRUE), length.out = 30L)) FW_data$Fecundity <- exp(predict(FW_mod, newdata = FW_data))  ggplot(data = fish, aes(x = Weight, y = Fecundity)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +   geom_line(data = FW_data, col = "blue") +   expand_limits(y = 0) tidy(FW_mod, conf.int = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   term        estimate std.error statistic  p.value conf.low conf.high #>   <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 (Intercept)    7.32     0.0537     136.  1.63e-77    7.22      7.43  #> 2 log(Weight)    0.868    0.0464      18.7 5.82e-27    0.775     0.961

title: Kootenay Lake Data
text: kootlake R package annual Rainbow Trout, Bull Trout Kokanee datasets Kootenay Lake. data provided Ministry Forests, Lands Natural Resources Operations, British Columbia. provides data set individual fish length, weight, sex fecundity information. well data sets annual counts species (bulltrout, kokanee gerrard) annual fishery fishery_catch information Kootenay Lake Rainbow Trout mail survey (KLRT).
code: library(tibble) library(kootlake) kootlake::fish #> # A tibble: 1,843 x 12 #>     Year Month   Day Species Length Weight Sex   Fecundity Location SampleID #>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>    <dbl>  <dbl> <chr>     <dbl> <chr>       <dbl> #>  1  1966     1     2 RB         580    2.8 <NA>         NA <NA>          113 #>  2  1966     1     5 RB         650    3.6 <NA>         NA <NA>          165 #>  3  1966     1     7 RB         420    0.8 <NA>         NA <NA>          136 #>  4  1966     1     8 RB         600    3.1 <NA>         NA <NA>          151 #>  5  1966     1     8 RB         630    3.6 <NA>         NA <NA>          131 #>  6  1966     1    15 RB         710    4.6 <NA>         NA <NA>          163 #>  7  1966     1    18 RB         737    6.4 <NA>         NA <NA>          107 #>  8  1966     1    18 RB         620    3   <NA>         NA <NA>          149 #>  9  1966     1    22 RB         500    1.7 <NA>         NA <NA>          144 #> 10  1966     1    30 RB         680    5.2 <NA>         NA <NA>          117 #> # … with 1,833 more rows, and 2 more variables: Source <chr>, Comment <chr>

title: Bull Trout Counts — bulltrout
text: annual resistivity counter redd counts Kaslo Keen Creeks.

title: Bull Trout Counts — bulltrout
text: tbl data frame: Year year (int). KasloRedds Kaslo Creek redd count (int). KeenRedds Keen Creek redd count (int). KasloCounter Kaslo Creek resistivity counter count (int).

title: Rainbow Trout data — fish
text: Data individual Rainbow Trout lakes rivers.

title: Rainbow Trout data — fish
text: data.frame Year year (int). Month month (int). Day day (int). Species Species code eg. "RB" (factor, 1 level). Length Fork length mm (int). Weight Weight kg (int). Sex Sex, "male" "female" (factor, 2 levels). Fecundity number eggs (int). Location Lake River (factor, 2 levels) SampleID Sample ID Source source data (char). Comment comments observations year date ranges (char).

title: Kootenay Lake Fishery — fishery
text: Kootenay Lake fishery data.

title: Kootenay Lake Fishery — fishery
text: data.frame: StartYear start year angling season runs April March (int). EndYear end year angling season runs April March (int). Licences number KLRT licenses sold (int). Mailings Number end season surveys mailed licence holders (int). Returns Number filled surveys returned (int). AnglerHours estimated number angler hours expended anglers purchased Kootenay Lake Rainbow Trout (KLRT) Conservation Surcharge License (int).

title: Fishery Catch Counts — fishery_catch
text: Harvested Released counts Bull Rainbow Trout

title: Fishery Catch Counts — fishery_catch
text: data.frame StartYear start year angling season (int). EndYear end year angling season (int). Harvested Estimated count individuals harvested (int). Released Estimated count individuals released (int). MinWeight Bottom weight range (int). MaxWeight Top weight range (int). Species Species, RB BT (factor. 2 levels).

title: Gerrard Escapement — gerrard
text: Gerrard Rainbow Trout spawner counts.

title: Gerrard Escapement — gerrard
text: data.frame: Year year spawner count (int). PeakCount peak count (int). FishDays total number fish days (int). FishRemoved total number fish removed prior spawning.

title: Gerrard Escapement — gerrard
text: total escapement can estimated multiplying peak count 3.08 dividing total number fish days residence time (currently) assumed 11.9 days.

title: Estimate Missing Values — kl_estimate_na
text: Estimates missing variable values predictor using linear model.

title: Estimate Missing Values — kl_estimate_na
text: x data frame columns variable predictor. variable string name variable column. predictor string name predictor column.

title: Estimate Missing Values — kl_estimate_na
text: original data frame missing variable values replaced estimated value.

title: Kokanee Escapement — kokanee
text: Kokanee escapement.

title: Kokanee Escapement — kokanee
text: data.frame: Year year (int). Lardeau Lardeau River escapement (int). MeadowCreek Meadow Creek escapement  (int). Fecundity mean number eggs per female  (int). LengthMale mean male spawner fork length mm (int). LengthFemale mean female spawner fork length mm (int).

title: Kootenay Lake Data — kootlake
text: Annual Rainbow Trout, Bull Trout Kokanee datasets Kootenay Lake.

title: Kootenay Lake Data — kootlake
text: includes `kl_estimate_na()` function estimate missing variable values predictor using linear model. default estimates missing Bull Trout redd counts Kaslo Creek using resistivity counter counts.

title: kootlake 0.3.1.9001
text: Add 2020 Kaslo River redd data Added 2020 gerrard peak counts fish days. Added 1957 1960 gerrard peak count. Adjusted 2019 gerrard peak count. Adjusted 2018 2019 gerrard fish days. Deprecate kl_estimate_na() using lifecycle.
